criteria.Details: Runtime database detected a corrupt SQLite image, archived the corrupt artifact, and rebuilt a writable runtime store.
Rollback mode restored the pre-update payload from local update transaction backup metadata.
A malformed content package was rejected, and disk-time malware blocking remained operational without requiring full uninstall.
Minifilter service is present with state stopped, enabling rollback/repair orchestration paths.

additionalPhase4Checks.name: Phase 4 patch-policy roundtrip
additionalPhase4Checks.details: Patch policy persisted maintenance windows, reboot grace, provider toggles, and feature deferral settings.

additionalPhase4Checks.name: Phase 4 Windows patch-state refresh
additionalPhase4Checks.details: Patch orchestrator refreshed policy, reboot coordination, recipe catalog, and local patch inventory without execution errors.

additionalPhase4Checks.name: Phase 4 software patch recipe coverage
additionalPhase4Checks.details: Phase 4 recipe catalog covers the initial high-risk household software baseline including browsers, reader, archive, runtime, conferencing, and media tooling.

additionalPhase4Checks.name: Phase 4 patch visibility snapshot
additionalPhase4Checks.details: Patch snapshot preserved failed updates, reboot-required state, manual-only software, unsupported software, and patch history for user-facing reporting.

additionalPhase4Checks.name: Phase 4 release-gate blocker enforcement
additionalPhase4Checks.details: Updater promotion-gate checks blocked all staged stable-channel manifests that violated pass-rate, risk-budget, hotfix, or approval-ticket requirements.
